scope: smallest read-only fixture-backed Atlas connection preview; no broad frontend refactor; no new automation
sections_added: Research question header with purpose, asset affordance tags, and readiness verdict
Source health panel with status counts, acquisition/parser notes, quality gates, and blocked/dirty/failed reasons
Evidence cluster panel with maturity, synthesis readiness, relationship density, source diversity, and orphan counts
Evidence atom cards with maturity, purpose status, stance counts, source count, asset tags, why clustered, and why weak
Relationship view with support, contradiction, qualification, definition, and scope-difference explanations
Evidence trace detail panel with source status, claim summary, atom/concept/relationship/cluster links, and public-safe connection explanation
Gaps / next move panel with readiness statuses, graph warnings, next recommended packet, and recommender reason

frontend_readiness_findings.notes: The route now makes the fixture-backed graph understandable and the public-site build passes. It remains a debug preview, not a polished graph visualization.

graph_observability_findings.notes: A human can follow the path from question to purpose tags, source health, atom cards, relationships, cluster metrics, trace details, warnings, and next packet.

local_model.reason: Purpose gates and typed fixture traces are visible, but arbitrary live Ollama source runs remain unproven.

paid_api.reason: No paid provider, cloud model, or escalation route is wired.

frontend_public_display.reason: The preview is static and safe for debugging, but fixture labels and readiness warnings must remain visible.

automation.reason: Acquisition/parser failures and live-source purpose enforcement remain blockers.

screenshot_manual_inspection_notes.manual_inspection: Static build generated /atlas-preview; targeted golden test verified exported HTML contains Tiny Atlas sections and next recommended packet.

verification.result: Next static build completed and exported /atlas-preview

top_blockers: Relationship density is fixture-proven, not arbitrary live research proof.
Purpose gates are not yet enforced across every future live arbitrary-source path.
Acquisition/parser status is still partially report-derived rather than consistently persisted into source metadata.
Paid API and automation readiness remain NO-GO.

next_recommended_packets.title: Live/Arbitrary Source Purpose Gate Enforcement
next_recommended_packets.goal: Apply purpose gates to staged live discover/fetch/ingest/extract/link/build/detect paths.

next_recommended_packets.title: Relationship Type Contract Formalization
next_recommended_packets.goal: Promote relationship type metadata into a validated contract/enum and update typed graph tests.

next_recommended_packets.title: Source Health Persistence for Atlas Preview
next_recommended_packets.goal: Persist acquisition/parser status and quality gate outcomes into Atlas-safe source health fields.
